zotero.abstractNote: The Muon optimizer has recently emerged as a powerful alternative to Adam for training large-scale neural networks, demonstrating superior performance in various settings. However, Muon's reliance on frequent singular value decomposition (SVD) operations can be computationally expensive, and its sensitivity to hyperparameter settings remains a challenge. In this work, we introduce Muown, a novel variant of Muon that replaces the costly SVD-based update with a more efficient row-norm control mechanism. We provide a theoretical analysis of the convergence properties of Muown and demonstrate its effectiveness through extensive empirical evaluations on large-scale language model training tasks. Our results show that Muown achieves comparable or better performance than Muon while significantly reducing the computational overhead, making it a more practical choice for training large-scale models.
